reason: The Hebrews in this text fragment refer to the descendants of Jacob, who later became known as the Israelites. They are depicted as being in a foreign land (Egypt) during a time of famine, seeking food to survive. The leadership mentioned includes Jacob (Israel) as the patriarch of the family, who instructs his sons to go to Egypt. Although the text does not specifically mention elders as the sole leadership, the reference to Jacob and his sons can be seen as a form of leadership within the family.
